title: Variables 2
description: Rust requires that variables have a value when they are used. This exercise explores type annotations.
instructions: Make the code compile by giving the variable a type annotation or a value.
hints: Variables in Rust must be initialized before they are used.
You need to assign a value to x when declaring it.
Try: let x = 10; or let x: i32 = 10;
explanation: In Rust, you can optionally annotate variables with a type using the syntax `let x: Type = value`. When you don't annotate the type, Rust infers it from the assigned value. However, a variable must always be initialized before use.
